title: Rivian Q4 2025 Earnings: Production Increase and R2 Launch Impact
text: Rivian tops fourth-quarter expectations, targets significant production increase heading into 2026. The company reported strong quarterly results driven by the early success of R2 pre-orders and improved manufacturing efficiency. Full year 2025 revenue was $5.2 billion, showing improvement on 2024's $4.97 billion. The company achieved additional gross margin improvements in Q4 2025. R1 cost per vehicle reached record lows, demonstrating manufacturing excellence. R2 pre-order demand exceeded management expectations by 30%. The company projects 2026 deliveries between 62,000 and 67,000 vehicles, up 47-59% from 2025. Q4 2025 earnings call emphasized R2 production readiness and Georgia facility progress. Management reaffirmed R2 customer deliveries commencing in Q2 2026. Cash position improved to $5.3 billion after receiving additional Volkswagen tranches. The company reduced adjusted EBITDA losses to a range of $1.7 billion to $1.9 billion for 2025, demonstrating improving trajectory. Analyst sentiment upgraded post-earnings with multiple price target increases. The market viewed Q4 results as validation of Rivian's strategic direction and execution capability.
